methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Hippocratic AI, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Munjal Shah, is evaluated against a peer set of 5 same-stage private comparables in the AI sector, supplemented by public reference points including Palantir (PLTR). The peer universe ranges from $500M (Photoroom) to $32.0B (Safe Superintelligence), with a median valuation of $5.0B across the sample.

Two triangulation methods are applied. The stage-progression method uses Hippocratic's $120M total raised as a base proxy, applying a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, yielding $228M. The peer-median method directly benchmarks against the five same-sector, same-stage comparables, producing $5.0B. This wide methodological divergence reflects meaningful heterogeneity within frontier AI at Series B—most acute between lower-funded software plays and high-intensity frontier model developers commanding substantial capital and valuation multiples.

The triangulated range spans $194M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.6B. The median reflects a position between stage-progression mechanics and peer comparables but remains heavily sensitive to company-specific execution risk, capital efficiency, and technical differentiation relative to the outlier valuations observed in the peer set. Key uncertainties include the stability of frontier AI valuations given rapid technical iteration and fundraising velocity, and whether Hippocratic's actual traction and burn profile align more closely with lower-funded or higher-funded peer comparables.
